description: FRONTIER: one timestamp function truncates to whole seconds for a downstream contract; siblings keep millisecond precision. v0.1 detects only sort-divergence, so it misses this.
inspired_by: Precision-mismatch bugs between millisecond and unix-second timestamp stores

findings.rationale: stampLedger shares the shape of stampEvent and stampMetric but truncates to whole seconds via Math.floor(t/1000)*1000. The truncation is part of the settlement-system contract. A timestamp-truncation detector should flag the divergent arithmetic; v0.1 (sort-only) cannot.
